name: Cycling Lane Pinares de Aznalcázar - Puebla del Río – circular
name_original: Carril Cicloturista Pinares de Aznalcázar - Puebla del Río – circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar traverse a varied landscape, connecting the Sierra Morena with the Doñana National Park. The region is characterized by Mediterranean forests, expansive pastures, olive groves, and cereal fields. As the corridor approaches Doñana, the scenery transitions to sandy areas, pine woods, and lush riverine forests, with wetlands and rice fields further downstream. The Guadiamar River is a central feature, offering mostly flat terrain suitable for touring cyclists, though some segments may be unpaved. Best touring cycling routes in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar The most popular touring cycling route is Protected Landscape Corredor Verde del Río Guadiamar, a 45.4 miles (73.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the ecological corridor, offering diverse natural scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cycling Lane Pinares de Aznalcázar - Puebla del Río – circular, a moderate 20.0 miles (32.2 km) path. This route features pine woods and riverine landscapes near Aznalcázar. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular, a 47.2 miles (76.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is defined by riverine forests, Mediterranean woodlands, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult routes with greater distances. The routes in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: There are over 130 touring cycling routes available in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You can find routes ranging from easy to difficult, with varying distances and elevations.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cyclists in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: The terrain in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is diverse, transitioning from Mediterranean forests and pastures to olive groves and cereal fields. As you approach the Doñana area, you'll encounter sandy areas, pine woods, and lush riverine forests. The routes are mostly flat, making them accessible for many cyclists, though some segments may be unpaved, suggesting suitability for mountain bikes with gears.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: Yes, the region offers several family-friendly options. With 17 easy routes and 66 moderate routes, many paths are suitable for families. The mostly flat terrain and dedicated cycling paths, free from motorized traffic, contribute to a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while cycling in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: The Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is rich in natural beauty. You'll cycle alongside the Guadiamar River, through Mediterranean forests, expansive pastures, and olive groves. Closer to Doñana, expect to see pine woods, riverine forests, wetlands, and rice fields. The area is also known for its diverse birdlife, including species like the black kite and Montagu's harrier, especially in nature reserves like Dehesa de Abajo.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can explore several points of interest. Notable attractions include the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1824240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado del Quema Shrine&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a great photo opportunity. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa of the Guadiamar&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor Protected Landscape&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6379194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Ford&lt;/a&gt; is another significant natural feature.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available for touring cyclists. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988507313/cycling-lane-pinares-de-aznalcazar-puebla-del-rio-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycling Lane Pinares de Aznalcázar - Puebla del Río – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through pine woods and riverine landscapes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990379889/embalse-de-agrio-de-aznalcollar-from-el-castillo-de-las-guardias-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and significant elevation.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: The Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is generally pleasant for cycling outside of the peak summer months. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and lush landscapes, making them ideal. Winter can also be suitable, though some days might be cooler. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What should I pack for a touring cycling trip in this region?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for unpaved segments, it's advisable to pack a touring or mountain bike with gears. Essential items include water, snacks, sun protection, a basic repair kit, and appropriate clothing layers. If you plan to explore off-road sections, consider sturdy tires. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views and wildlife!

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every route, many towns and villages along the corridor, such as Aznalcázar, Gerena, and Huévar, offer public parking options. It's recommended to check the starting points of individual routes on komoot for more precise parking information, as some trailheads may have designated spaces.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: The Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse natural scenery, the peaceful dedicated cycling paths, and the opportunity to observe wildlife. The connection between Sierra Morena and Doñana, offering a unique ecological experience, is also a frequently highlighted aspect.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988535297/protected-landscape-corredor-verde-del-rio-guadiamar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Protected Landscape Corredor Verde del Río Guadiamar&lt;/a&gt; is a 73 km trail with diverse natural scenery. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Agrio Reservoir – Charcofrío loop from Aznalcóllar&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes over its 67 km distance.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while touring cycling in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: Absolutely. The Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is designed to facilitate the movement of wild fauna between Sierra Morena and Doñana, enhancing the chances of wildlife observation. The area is particularly renowned for its diverse birdlife, including species like the black kite and Montagu's harrier, especially in protected areas and wetlands. Keep an eye out for various mammals and reptiles as well.
tips: The heart of the Pine Forests of Aznalcázar - one of the forest areas of greatest ecological interest within the province of Seville. Dense forests, mainly made up of pinyon pine, make this place an obligatory stopover for migrating birds and a habitat for many more species.
A set of stone pines (Pinus pinea) 25 meters or more tall, which stand out among other specimens of the same species.
An obligatory stop for breakfast or lunch after the hiking or cycling route in the Pinar de Aznalcázar. Site surrounded by trees, very quiet. It is right next to Camping Dehesa Nueva.
